roles.core: central bot, D1 authority, Marzban credential isolation, inbound tag resolution, ownership ledger and update controller
roles.edge: central web dashboard plus reseller bots and shared Marzban service lifecycle
roles.processor: scheduled shared Marzban usage polling, automatic debits, health, backups and background automation

features: Marzban inbound configuration accepts the username of one existing template subscription instead of manual Proxies/Inbounds JSON
the template subscription is read through GET /api/user/{username}; its inbound mappings are copied while UUIDs and passwords are never reused
VLESS flow and Shadowsocks method may be retained, while credential fields are deliberately omitted so Marzban generates fresh credentials
Marzban URLs entered with or without /dashboard are normalized to the API base
the central web dashboard and central Telegram bot expose a manager-only /dashboard login link
resellers never receive the Marzban dashboard URL, administrator credentials, token or template account details
all per-reseller usage billing and service ownership features remain included

deployment.automatic_provisioning: upload the ZIP through the central bot
deployment.marzban_url_input: accepts https://host or https://host/dashboard and stores normalized API base
deployment.manager_dashboard: normalized API base + /dashboard/; visible only in central management interfaces
deployment.template_input: exact username of an existing Marzban account whose proxies/inbounds should be used as the service template

validation.worker_node_syntax: passed for core, edge and processor
validation.embedded_browser_scripts: 8 script blocks extracted and passed node --check
validation.dashboard_url_normalization: passed for base URLs and URLs ending in /dashboard or /dashboard/
validation.template_credential_isolation: passed; UUIDs, passwords and unsupported fields were not copied
validation.live_marzban_connection: not run because production credentials were not provided

limitations: the template username must identify an existing Marzban account visible to the configured shared administrator
the template account must have at least one supported active inbound
live validation requires production Marzban credentials and network access
supported automatically generated proxy types are VLESS, VMess, Trojan and Shadowsocks
